message:
fix(analytics): use word boundaries for command categorization

Improves command categorization accuracy with regex word boundaries:

Before: cmd.includes("test") matched "latest", "greatest", etc.
After: /\btest\b/.test(cmd) only matches standalone "test" word

Patterns updated:
- build_test: test, build, compile now use \b boundaries
- search: search, glob now use \b boundaries
- read: read now uses \b boundary
- write: write, edit, patch now use \b boundaries
- execute: bash now uses \b boundary

This prevents false positives like:
- "download latest" being categorized as build_test
- "bread recipe" being categorized as read
- "patchwork" being categorized as write

Shell command patterns (npm, bun, cat, sed, rg, find) use explicit
space delimiters which already prevent false matches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
